task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application window is open with the 'dwanko_run' scene loaded, visible in the title bar, Xsheet, or Timeline.

task_completion.description: Create a second layer (column) containing the character animation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Xsheet or Timeline shows two columns/layers populated with the same drawing sequence.

task_completion.description: Offset the timing of the second layer by exactly 4 frames behind the original.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Xsheet or Timeline, the drawings in the second column start exactly 4 frames lower (later) than the first column.

task_completion.description: Reduce the opacity of the second layer to approximately 50%.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The viewer shows a semi-transparent 'ghost' of the character trailing the main character, or the opacity/transparency setting for the duplicated column/level is visibly set to around 50%.

task_completion.description: Set the output format to PNG and the output directory to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/echo_effect/'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog is open, showing the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/echo_effect/' and the file format set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Render the animation to generate the PNG frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar/window is visible in OpenToonz, or a file manager/terminal shows the newly generated PNG files in the target directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz graphical interface to perform the edits.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenToonz GUI (Xsheet, Output Settings, etc.) rather than modifying the .tnz XML file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent rendered the output directly from OpenToonz.
integrity.visual_evidence: The output files are generated via the OpenToonz render process (e.g., render dialog visible) rather than using terminal tools like ImageMagick or ffmpeg to fake the visual echo effect.

integrity.description: The agent used OpenToonz to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application window is clearly visible and actively used for the task, not another animation software.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target scene is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The second layer must be offset by exactly 4 frames. The opacity of the second layer must be between 30% and 70% (target 50%). The output must be rendered as PNG frames to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/echo_effect', generating at least 10 files.
